content.text:
Based on my analysis of the chart:

<think>
A "timeless" name - one consistently given over a long period - would manifest as a **high Interquartile Range (IQR)** on the y-axis. A high IQR means the central 50% of living people with that name span many decades of birth years, indicating the name was popular across multiple generations.

Looking at the chart:
- **Lucille** sits highest on the y-axis (IQR ≈ 65), in the top-right area of the plot
- **Eleanor** is next highest (IQR ≈ 60), in the top-left
- **Hazel** and **Josephine** are also high but slightly lower

Lucille has the highest IQR value (~65), meaning the span between its 25th and 75th percentile ages is the widest — approximately 65 years. This indicates people named Lucille range from relatively young to very old, suggesting the name has been consistently given across many decades.
</think>

<answer>
Lucille

It has the highest Interquartile Range (IQR ≈ 65) on the chart, meaning the central 50% of living people named Lucille span roughly 65 years of ages. This wide spread indicates the name has been consistently given across many generations — making it the most timeless by the given definition.
</answer>
